stemHtml: During cruise, after ATC handover, a pilot receives a route clearance up to a waypoint at an adjacent controlled airspace. While approaching the clearance limit waypoint, what must the pilot do?

options.a: Perform a lateral offset from the filed flight-plan route, maintaining the frequency of the ATC unit which has provided him/her the last clearance.
options.b: Expect the appropriate ATC unit to Issue an amended clearance to the destination aerodrome, 5 minutes prior to reaching the clearance limit.
options.c: Expect the appropriate ATC unit to Issue an amended clearance to the destination aerodrome, as soon as practicable.
options.d: Change frequency to the appropriate. ATC unit on his/her own initiative, and provide a full position report.
